Title: Retrieve server CapabilityStatement via metadata endpoint
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable at /ws/fhir2/R4; valid bearer token available
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET to /ws/fhir2/R4/metadata with Accept application/fhir+json | 2. Inspect HTTP status | 3. Inspect resourceType and fhirVersion fields
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 returned; body resourceType is CapabilityStatement; fhirVersion is 4.0.1; rest.mode is server

Title: CapabilityStatement lists supported Patient resource interactions
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable; metadata accessible
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/metadata | 2. Locate rest.resource entry where type=Patient | 3. Verify interaction array | 4. Verify searchParam list
Expected_Result: Patient resource entry present listing read, search-type, create, update interactions and searchParams including _id, identifier, name, birthdate

Title: Server declares FHIR version 4.0.1 conformance
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/metadata | 2. Read fhirVersion field | 3. Compare against R4 release
Expected_Result: fhirVersion equals 4.0.1 confirming HL7 FHIR R4 conformance

Title: Server returns application/fhir+json content type
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable; a known Patient id exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} with Accept application/fhir+json | 2. Inspect Content-Type response header
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Content-Type response header is application/fhir+json

Title: Server honors _format=json query parameter override
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable; known Patient id exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id}?_format=json with no Accept header | 2. Inspect Content-Type
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; response body serialized as FHIR JSON regardless of Accept header

Title: Unsupported Accept type returns 406 Not Acceptable
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable; known Patient id exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} with Accept application/xml+badtype | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 406 Not Acceptable returned; no resource body leaked

Title: Read existing Patient by id returns correct resource
Preconditions: A Patient with known uuid exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Verify resourceType and id | 3. Verify name and gender fields
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resourceType Patient; id matches request; name and gender populated from stored record

Title: Read non-existent Patient id returns 404 with OperationOutcome
Preconditions: Valid token; a uuid that does not exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{nonexistent} | 2. Inspect status | 3. Inspect body
Expected_Result: HTTP 404; body resourceType OperationOutcome with issue.severity error and issue.code not-found

Title: Read Patient with malformed id syntax returns 400 or 404
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/!!invalid## | 2. Inspect status and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 or 404 with OperationOutcome; server does not 500

Title: Create Patient with valid required fields
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege; Content-Type application/fhir+json
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with valid Patient body | 2. Inspect status | 3. Inspect Location header | 4. GET the created resource
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 Created; Location header points to new Patient; resource retrievable with server-assigned id

Title: Create Patient missing required name returns 400 OperationOutcome
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with body lacking name element | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 (or 422); OperationOutcome with issue describing missing required field; no resource created

Title: Create Patient with invalid gender code returns 400
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with gender=robot | 2. Inspect status and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome flags invalid code for AdministrativeGender value set

Title: Create Patient with malformed JSON returns 400
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with truncated JSON body | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome indicates parse/structure error; server does not 500

Title: Create with resourceType mismatch to endpoint returns 400
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with body resourceType Observation | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome reports resource type mismatch; no resource created

Title: Update existing Patient via PUT with matching id
Preconditions: An existing Patient with known id; valid token with edit privilege
Test_Steps: 1. GET current Patient | 2. Modify name.given | 3. PUT /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} with full updated body including id | 4. GET to confirm
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; updated field persisted; resource version/lastUpdated advanced

Title: PUT with body id mismatching URL id returns 400
Preconditions: An existing Patient; valid token with edit privilege
Test_Steps: 1. PUT /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{idA} with body containing id idB | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome reports id mismatch; no cross-record overwrite

Title: Delete existing Patient returns success and resource no longer readable
Preconditions: An existing test Patient; valid token with delete privilege
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect status | 3. GET same id
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 or 204; subsequent GET returns 404 or 410 Gone

Title: Delete non-existent Patient returns 404 OperationOutcome
Preconditions: Valid token with delete privilege
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{nonexistent} | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 404; OperationOutcome not-found returned

Title: Search Patient by _id returns single matching bundle
Preconditions: A Patient with known uuid exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_id={id} | 2. Inspect bundle type and total | 3. Verify entry
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle type searchset; total 1; entry contains Patient with matching id

Title: Search Patient by identifier system and value
Preconditions: A Patient with known identifier exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?identifier={system}|{value} | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset bundle containing the Patient whose identifier matches system and value

Title: Search Patient by family name returns matches
Preconditions: Patients with family name Tester exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?family=Tester | 2. Inspect bundle entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset bundle; all entries have a name with family Tester

Title: Search Patient by name token does partial match
Preconditions: Patients with name containing Ada exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?name=Ada | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset bundle; entries include patients whose given or family name contains Ada

Title: Search Patient by birthdate exact match
Preconditions: Patient born 1990-01-15 exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?birthdate=1990-01-15 | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries all have birthDate 1990-01-15

Title: Search Patient by birthdate ge/le range prefixes
Preconditions: Patients across date range exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?birthdate=ge1990-01-01&birthdate=le1990-12-31 | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries have birthDate within 1990 inclusive of boundary dates

Title: Search with no matching criteria returns empty searchset
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?family=Zzqxwv | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle type searchset; total 0; empty or absent entry array

Title: Unknown search parameter is rejected or ignored per server policy
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?nonsenseparam=foo | 2. Inspect status and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with OperationOutcome, or 200 with parameter ignored and self link reflecting handling

Title: Search result bundle includes self and next links when paged
Preconditions: More patients than page size exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=10 | 2. Inspect bundle.link rel values | 3. Follow next link
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; bundle.link contains self and next; following next returns subsequent page bundle

Title: _count honored to limit entries per page
Preconditions: At least 5 patients exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=2 | 2. Count entries returned
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entry array length is at most 2; bundle.total reflects full match count

Title: _count=0 returns count-only summary bundle
Preconditions: Patients exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=0 | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; bundle.total populated; no resource entries returned

Title: Negative _count value rejected with 400
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=-5 | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 OperationOutcome, or coerced safe default; never returns 500

Title: Read existing Encounter by id
Preconditions: An Encounter with known id exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Encounter/{id} | 2. Verify resourceType, status, subject reference
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resourceType Encounter; subject references the patient; status populated

Title: Search Encounter by patient reference
Preconditions: Patient with encounters exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Encounter?patient={patientId} | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset bundle; all entries reference the requested patient as subject

Title: Search Encounter by date range
Preconditions: Encounters across dates exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Encounter?date=ge2026-01-01&date=le2026-06-30 | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries have period within the requested range

Preconditions: An Encounter referencing a Patient exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET Encounter | 2. Extract subject.reference | 3. GET referenced Patient
Expected_Result: Subject reference resolves to HTTP 200 Patient with matching id; no dangling reference

Title: Read existing Observation by id
Preconditions: An Observation with known id exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation/{id} | 2. Verify code, value, subject
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resourceType Observation; code and value[x] populated; subject reference present

Preconditions: Observations for a patient with known LOINC code exist; valid token

Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries match both patient and the heart rate LOINC code

Title: Search Observation by category vital-signs
Preconditions: Vital sign observations exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation?category=vital-signs&patient={id} | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries carry category coding vital-signs

Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation with status, code, subject, valueQuantity | 2. Verify 201 | 3. GET created resource
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; Observation persisted with submitted code and value retrievable by id

Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation omitting required status | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400/422; OperationOutcome flags missing required status element

Title: Create Observation with extreme numeric value boundary
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST Observation with valueQuantity at very large magnitude | 2. Inspect status | 3. GET to confirm precision
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 with value stored without overflow, OR 400 if server enforces a documented range; no silent truncation

Title: Read existing Condition by id

Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Condition/{id} | 2. Verify code, clinicalStatus, subject

Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries reference patient and carry clinicalStatus active

Title: Create Condition with coded diagnosis

Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Condition with code, clinicalStatus, subject | 2. Verify 201
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; Condition persisted with submitted SNOMED code and subject

Expected_Result: HTTP 200; clinicalStatus now resolved; transition persisted and historically consistent

Title: Read existing AllergyIntolerance by id

Test_Steps: 1. POST AllergyIntolerance with code, patient, criticality high, reaction | 2. Verify 201
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; allergy persisted with high criticality so downstream safety checks can fire

Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome flags value not in AllergyIntoleranceCriticality value set

Title: Read existing MedicationRequest by id
Preconditions: A MedicationRequest with known id exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/MedicationRequest/{id} | 2. Verify status, intent, medication, subject
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resourceType MedicationRequest; status, intent, medication and subject populated

Preconditions: Active medication orders exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/MedicationRequest?patient={id}&status=active | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries reference patient and carry status active

Title: Create MedicationRequest with valid status intent and medication

Test_Steps: 1. POST MedicationRequest with status, intent, medicationCodeableConcept, subject, requester | 2. Verify 201
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; order persisted and retrievable; references resolve

Expected_Result: HTTP 400/422; OperationOutcome reports unresolvable reference; order not created against phantom patient

Title: Request without credentials returns 401
Preconditions: No Authorization header supplied; FHIR endpoint reachable
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} with no Authorization header | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; no PHI returned in body

Title: Request with expired or invalid token returns 401
Preconditions: An expired/invalid bearer token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=1 with invalid token | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 401; OperationOutcome or WWW-Authenticate challenge; no PHI leaked

Title: User lacking privilege gets 403 on protected resource
Preconditions: A valid token for a user without Get Patients privilege
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} with under-privileged token | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden; OperationOutcome forbidden; resource not disclosed

Title: Read-only user cannot create resource (403)
Preconditions: A valid token for a read-only user
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with valid body using read-only token | 2. Inspect status
Test_Data: Read-only user token; valid Patient body
Expected_Result: HTTP 403; no resource created

Title: SQL injection style identifier search is neutralized
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?identifier=1%27%20OR%20%271%27%3D%271 | 2. Inspect status and result
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 empty searchset or 400; no mass disclosure; server logs no SQL error and does not 500

Title: Patient id traversal attempt does not access other resource types
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/../Observation/{id} | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400/404; no cross-resource access via path manipulation

Title: Read of Patient PHI generates audit log entry
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled; valid token; admin access to audit store
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Query audit/log store for the access event
Expected_Result: Audit record captured with actor, patient id, timestamp and action supporting HIPAA access traceability

Title: Created Patient persists across server restart
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege; ability to restart test instance
Test_Steps: 1. POST create Patient | 2. Restart FHIR/server instance | 3. GET created Patient by id
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 then after restart HTTP 200 with same data confirming durable persistence

Title: Concurrent updates use version-aware optimistic locking
Preconditions: An existing Patient with ETag/versionId; valid tokens
Test_Steps: 1. GET Patient capture ETag | 2. PUT with If-Match stale version | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 409 or 412 precondition failed on stale version; lost-update prevented

Title: Search bundle entry includes fullUrl and search mode match
Preconditions: Patients exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=2 | 2. Inspect each entry fullUrl and entry.search.mode
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; each entry has absolute fullUrl and search.mode match per FHIR bundle structure

Title: Transaction bundle rolls back fully on one entry failure
Preconditions: Server supports transaction bundles; valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST transaction Bundle with one valid and one invalid resource entry | 2. Inspect status | 3. Confirm neither persisted
Test_Data: Bundle.type=transaction; entry1 valid Patient; entry2 invalid Observation
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome; atomic rollback so the valid entry is also not created

Title: Server error responses always carry OperationOutcome body
Preconditions: Valid token
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a 400 via invalid create | 2. Trigger a 404 via missing read | 3. Inspect both bodies
Expected_Result: Both responses contain resourceType OperationOutcome with issue.severity and issue.code; consistent error contract

Title: Reject Patient with multiple values where cardinality is 0..1
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST Patient with two birthDate values | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome flags cardinality violation for 0..1 element

Title: Accept Patient with multiple given names (0..*)
Preconditions: Valid token with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST Patient with name.given array of two values | 2. Verify 201 | 3. GET to confirm both retained
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; both given names persisted reflecting 0..* cardinality

Title: Combined name plus gender plus birthdate search
Preconditions: Patients matching combinations exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?name=Ada&gender=female&birthdate=1990-01-15 | 2. Inspect entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries satisfy every supplied parameter (AND semantics)

Title: Gender search value mapping decision table
Preconditions: Patients of each gender exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET Patient?gender=male | 2. GET ?gender=female | 3. GET ?gender=other | 4. GET ?gender=unknown | 5. Compare each result set
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 each; every entry matches the requested AdministrativeGender value; no cross-leak between buckets

Title: Search with _summary=count returns total only
Preconditions: Patients exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_summary=count | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; bundle.total present; no entry resources returned

Title: Search by _lastUpdated returns recently modified resources
Preconditions: Recently updated patients exist; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_lastUpdated=ge2026-06-01 | 2. Inspect meta.lastUpdated of entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries have meta.lastUpdated on or after the boundary

Title: Unsupported HTTP method PATCH handled gracefully

Expected_Result: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed or 415 with OperationOutcome; server does not 500

Title: HEAD request to read endpoint returns headers without body
Preconditions: Known Patient id exists; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. Send HEAD /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect status and body length
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with headers and empty body, or 405 if unsupported; consistent and not 500

Title: Read historical version via _history when supported
Preconditions: A Patient that has been updated at least once; valid token

Expected_Result: HTTP 200; history bundle lists versions; specific version retrievable, or 404 if vread unsupported per CapabilityStatement

Title: Resource Adapter maps OpenEMR identifier to FHIR Patient.identifier

Expected_Result: HTTP 200; OpenEMR MRN exposed as FHIR Patient.identifier with correct system URI via adapter

Title: HAPI FHIR backend returns conformant Bundle through adapter
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured against HAPI FHIR; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. Search Patient via adapter routed to HAPI FHIR | 2. Inspect bundle structure and fhirVersion
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset Bundle conformant to R4; adapter does not corrupt structure or links

Title: omiiCARE platform exposes Observation via adapter consistently

Title: HL7 v2 ADT ingest reflected in FHIR Patient read

Title: Search results sortable by birthdate descending

Title: _revinclude pulls Observations for searched Patient
